strategy_research: {"strategies": [{"name": "Antonacci Global Equity Momentum (GEM)", "description": "Each month, apply two momentum tests: (1) absolute \u2014 did SPY beat T-Bills over trailing 12 months? If no, hold AGG. (2) relative \u2014 if yes, did SPY beat EFA? If yes hold SPY, else hold EFA. ~1.5 trades/year.", "evidence": ["Antonacci, 'Risk Premia Harvesting Through Dual Momentum', SSRN 2042750 (2012) \u2014 won 2012 NAAIM Wagner Award", "Greyserman & Kaminski (2014): 12-month momentum lookback beat buy-and-hold since the 1600s", "Book: 'Dual Momentum Investing', McGraw-Hill (2014)", "ReSolve Asset Management independent replication: 'Global Equity Momentum: A Craftsman's Perspective'"], "historical_sharpe": "0.87 (1974\u20132013 backtest); ~0.65\u20130.75 in post-2000 ETF-era backtests", "market_conditions": "Works: bear markets, global equity rotations, volatile sideways markets. Fails: sustained US-only bull markets (2013\u20132021), simultaneous equity+bond bear markets (2022 was severe failure \u2014 AGG offered no refuge)", "instruments": ["SPY", "EFA", "AGG"], "timeframe": "monthly", "complexity": "low"}, {"name": "Keller Vigilant Asset Allocation (VAA-G4)", "description": "Monthly weighted momentum score = (12\u00d7p0/p1) + (4\u00d7p0/p3) + (2\u00d7p0/p6) + (p0/p12) \u2212 19 for each of 4 offensive ETFs (SPY, EFA, EEM, AGG). If ALL scores positive, buy top scorer. If ANY score is negative, buy top defensive ETF (LQD, IEF, or SHY). 100% concentrated single position.", "evidence": ["Keller & Keuning, 'Breadth Momentum and Vigilant Asset Allocation (VAA)', SSRN 3002624 (2017)", "Follow-on: 'Breadth Momentum and the Canary Universe: Defensive Asset Allocation (DAA)', SSRN 3212862 (2018)", "AllocateSmartly independent replication confirms backtested results", "QuantConnect community implementations validated across multiple market regimes"], "historical_sharpe": "0.94 (Dec 1970\u2013Dec 2016); MAR ratio 1.46; max drawdown only -13% vs -30% for 60/40", "market_conditions": "Works: volatile markets, bear markets, crash environments \u2014 early defensive trigger is key strength. Fails: strong sustained bull markets (high opportunity cost), choppy recovering markets (whipsaw risk from short-term momentum bias)", "instruments": ["SPY", "EFA", "IWM", "AGG", "TLT"], "timeframe": "monthly", "complexity": "low"}, {"name": "Cross-Asset Momentum + Trend Filter + Volatility Targeting", "description": "Score each ETF by (12-month return / 12-month volatility). Invest equal weight in top 3 ranked assets. Apply trend filter: any asset below its 10-month SMA is replaced with cash/BIL. Scale position sizes so portfolio targets ~10\u201312% annualized volatility.", "evidence": ["Asness, Moskowitz & Pedersen, 'Value and Momentum Everywhere', Journal of Finance Vol.68 No.3 (2013), SSRN 2174501", "Moskowitz, Ooi & Pedersen, 'Time Series Momentum', Journal of Financial Economics (2012), SSRN 2089463", "Hurst, Ooi & Pedersen (AQR), 'A Century of Evidence on Trend-Following', SSRN 2993026 (2017): Sharpe 1.16 over 1880\u20132013", "Clare et al., 'Risk Parity, Momentum and Trend Following in Global Asset Allocation', Journal of Behavioral and Experimental Finance (2016)", "Hanauer & Windm\u00fcller, 'Enhanced Momentum Strategies' (Robeco): risk management materially improves Sharpe for momentum"], "historical_sharpe": "0.78\u20131.16 depending on universe; diversified multi-asset TSMOM ~1.0\u20131.16 (AQR); EMV-weighted cross-asset ~0.78 (J.P. Morgan/CME 1972\u20132014)", "market_conditions": "Works: trending directional markets, globally diversified environments, CTA-style conditions. Fails: sharp momentum reversals (March 2020 V-shape), 'momentum crash' environments (2009 recovery), simultaneous bond+equity selloffs", "instruments": ["SPY", "QQQ", "IWM", "EFA", "TLT", "AGG", "GLD", "VTI"], "timeframe": "monthly", "complexity": "medium"}, {"name": "Regime-Based Rotation (VIX + Yield Curve + Trend)", "description": "Multi-signal regime classifier: if SPY > 10-month SMA AND yield curve (10Y\u20132Y) not inverted AND VIX < 25, hold risk assets. If any signal triggers defensive, rotate to TLT or BIL. Optional: use VIX EWMA trend to scale equity exposure continuously rather than binary switches.", "evidence": ["Faber, 'A Quantitative Approach to Tactical Asset Allocation', Journal of Wealth Management (2006/2009), SSRN 962461: equity-like returns with bond-like volatility", "arXiv 2410.14841 (2024): ML model using VIX + 2Y yield + yield curve slope achieves Sharpe 1.38 out-of-sample 2017\u20132022", "Nature Scientific Reports (2025): regime-switching with jump model and MPC validated over 2005\u20132022 including GFC and COVID", "Ilmanen, 'Expected Returns' (AQR, 2011): comprehensive macro-regime expected return framework"], "historical_sharpe": "0.76 (Faber 5-asset TAA 1973\u20132012); 1.38 out-of-sample (ML multi-signal, 2017\u20132022)", "market_conditions": "Works: orderly bear markets with lead time (2000\u20132002, 2008\u20132009), yield curve inversion \u2192 recession cycles (6\u201318 month lag). Fails: V-shaped recoveries (March 2020 \u2014 VIX spike reversed in weeks), 2022 simultaneous equity+bond selloff, yield curve false positives", "instruments": ["SPY", "TLT", "AGG", "GLD"], "timeframe": "monthly", "complexity": "medium"}, {"name": "Dual Momentum + Intermarket Divergence Filter (GEM Enhanced)", "description": "Run standard GEM (Antonacci) as the base strategy, but add a pre-trade filter: check GLD and TLT 200-day MA trends before executing each monthly signal. If GLD and TLT are both above their 200-day MAs simultaneously (unusual safe-haven flight), skip equity allocation and hold AGG/BIL regardless of GEM signal. Handles the 2022-style failure mode.", "evidence": ["Murphy, 'Intermarket Technical Analysis', Wiley/IFTA (1991): foundational four-asset-class correlation framework", "Murphy, 'Trading with Intermarket Analysis' (updated): documents 1998 deflationary regime shift in gold-bond correlations", "arXiv 2511.08571 (2025): quantitative gold futures strategy achieving out-of-sample Sharpe ~2.9 exploiting regime-consistent signals", "Emmrich & McGroarty (2013): ~10% gold allocation is optimal diversification weight; Sharpe improvement confirmed 1973\u20132024", "Proactive Advisor Magazine: 5\u201318% gold allocation improved 60/40 portfolio Sharpe (reference Sharpe 0.97) across 1973\u20132024"], "historical_sharpe": "GEM base: 0.87; with GLD intermarket filter estimated 0.90\u20131.00 (reduces 2022-style simultaneous drawdowns); Murphy's original signals remained valid 17+ years without re-optimization", "market_conditions": "Works: inflation regime shifts, macro turning points, crisis identification. Fails: short-term noise causes false divergence signals; all correlations converge to 1 in acute liquidity crises (2008, March 2020). Best as confirming overlay, not standalone.", "instruments": ["SPY", "EFA", "AGG", "GLD", "TLT"], "timeframe": "monthly", "complexity": "low"}, {"name": "Stacked Signal Ensemble Rotation", "description": "For each ETF in the universe, compute a composite score averaging three independently ranked signals: (1) 12-month return rank, (2) binary trend flag (1 if price > 200-day MA, else 0), (3) 3-month return rank. Equal-weight the three signal scores. Invest in top 2\u20133 ETFs by composite score each month; cash out any with composite score below median.", "evidence": ["Goldman Sachs Asset Management (2018): 'How to Combine Investment Signals in Long/Short Strategies' \u2014 integrated z-score combination is most practical for long-only rotation", "arXiv 2012.07149, Oxford (2020): ML ranking of cross-sectional ensemble provides ~3x Sharpe improvement vs. single signal", "L\u00f3pez de Prado, 'Advances in Financial Machine Learning', Wiley (2018): ensemble methods reduce both bias and variance in financial signal combination", "Hanauer & Windm\u00fcller (Robeco): all 8 of 103 strategies showing Sharpe improvement from risk management were momentum strategies with signal ensembles", "Equal-weighting signals is more robust out-of-sample than IC-weighted or variance-weighted combinations per multiple empirical studies"], "historical_sharpe": "Standalone momentum: 0.5\u20130.7; 3\u20135 signal ensemble: 0.7\u20131.0; ML cross-sectional ranking: ~3x single-signal Sharpe (Oxford study)", "market_conditions": "Works: liquid multi-asset universes, low signal cross-correlation environments. Fails: signal correlation spike (2022 all assets fell together), overfitting risk increases with signal count, small universes (8\u201312 ETFs) limit diversification of prediction error", "instruments": ["SPY", "QQQ", "IWM", "VTI", "GLD", "TLT", "AGG", "EFA"], "timeframe": "monthly", "complexity": "medium"}, {"name": "Hierarchical Risk Parity + Momentum Selection (HRP-Momentum Hybrid)", "description": "Two-stage process: (1) Selection \u2014 use 12-month momentum to identify which ETFs to hold (top 50% by return, all above 10-month SMA). (2) Sizing \u2014 apply HRP algorithm to size positions among selected ETFs using the trailing covariance matrix (no matrix inversion required). Monthly rebalance. Requires Python (PyPortfolioOpt library).", "evidence": ["L\u00f3pez de Prado, 'Building Diversified Portfolios that Outperform Out of Sample', Journal of Portfolio Management Vol.42 No.4 (2016), SSRN 2708678", "Book: 'Advances in Financial Machine Learning', Wiley (2018), Chapter 16", "Monte Carlo simulations in original paper: HRP out-of-sample variance 72.47% lower than CLA (Markowitz); out-of-sample Sharpe +31.3% vs. CLA", "HRP out-of-sample variance 38.24% lower than Inverse Variance Portfolio (IVP)", "Brazilian market study (FGV, Revista Brasileira de Finan\u00e7as): confirmed out-of-sample outperformance vs. equal weight and risk parity"], "historical_sharpe": "HRP standalone: +31.3% Sharpe improvement over Markowitz CLA (out-of-sample Monte Carlo); combined with momentum selection: estimated 0.95\u20131.10 based on component evidence", "market_conditions": "Works: large diverse multi-asset portfolios with meaningful correlation structure, ill-conditioned covariance matrices where Markowitz fails. Fails: small portfolios (3\u20135 assets) where clustering adds nothing over equal weight; correlation regime shifts (2022 stocks+bonds becoming positively correlated breaks cluster structure)", "instruments": ["SPY", "QQQ", "IWM", "VTI", "GLD", "TLT", "AGG", "EFA"], "timeframe": "monthly", "complexity": "high"}], "recommendation": "Start with VAA-G4 (Keller Vigilant Asset Allocation) for highest risk-adjusted performance with manageable complexity. VAA's Sharpe of 0.94 and max drawdown of only -13% (vs -30%+ for 60/40) are the best combination among low-complexity strategies, and its breadth momentum approach provides earlier defensive signals than GEM. Implement it with a simple monthly spreadsheet using SPY, EFA, AGG, TLT, IEF, and SHY from Yahoo Finance. Once comfortable, layer in the GLD intermarket divergence filter from Strategy 5 to reduce exposure to 2022-style simultaneous equity+bond drawdowns \u2014 this requires only two additional checks per month (GLD and TLT vs. their 200-day MAs) and addresses VAA's main structural vulnerability. The Stacked Signal Ensemble (Strategy 6) is the recommended second-phase upgrade: it improves on pure momentum by combining 3-month, 12-month, and trend signals with equal weighting, which academic evidence shows materially reduces prediction error variance without requiring any optimization. Avoid the January Effect and standalone Intermarket Divergence as primary strategies \u2014 both lack standalone statistical significance at retail scale. HRP-Momentum is the highest-ceiling approach but requires Python and is best deferred until the simpler strategies are running smoothly."}
